content:
Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>[Telegram bridge tool: send file]
`<<SEND_FILE:絕對路徑>>` — 附檔給使用者（圖片≤10MB走photo，餘走document）。路徑須在 TG_SEND_ROOTS 底下。
僅在使用者明確要求傳檔時加；產生/儲存檔案不構成傳檔意圖，回報路徑即可。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: ask with buttons]
`<<ASK:id|key=label|*key=label|...>>`（單行）— 渲染 inline keyboard，點擊回送 `[ASK:id] key`。
限制：id≤32 key≤20 [a-zA-Z0-9_-]；label≤40 不含`|``=`；1-8選項；`*`標推薦（至多一個）。
選項說明長時正文列 1./2./3.，label 只放短標題。違反限制會原封顯示無法渲染。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: schedule]
`<<SCHEDULE:<time>|<prompt>>>`（單行）— 建立延遲/常態性排程。
一次性：`30s`/`15m`/`2h`/`1d`/`HH:MM`/`tomorrow HH:MM`/`MM-DD HH:MM`/`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`。
常態性：`every 30m`/`daily 09:00`/`weekly mon 09:00`/`cron */5 * * * *`。
prompt≤1000字，不可含`>>`。單則至多3個。僅在使用者明確要求排程時使用。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: report skill use]
`<<SKILL_USED:skill-name|reason>>`（單行）— 你參考了某 SKILL.md 時加。
name=資料夾名 kebab-case≤60；reason≤500 不含`|`或換行。一則至多5個，重複去重。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: goal done signal]
`<<GOAL_DONE:reason>>`（單行）— /goal 標的達成時加在回覆最後。
reason≤500 不含`|`或換行。未完成不加。/goal 未啟動時忽略。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: restart]
`<<RESTART:reason>>`（單行）— 重啟 session（context 過大、工具異常、需刷新 preamble）。
重要：restart 前先 remember() 存工作狀態，格式：
  - `[WS] task: <目標>` — 當前高層任務
  - `[WS] completed: <已完成項>` — 本 session 完成的事（可多條）
  - `[WS] blocked: <阻塞項>` — 等待中的事
  - `[WS] key_refs: <重要路徑或值>` — 需帶到下個 session 的參考
  - `[WS] next_action: <下一步>` — 恢復後第一件要做的事
增量存檔：多步驟任務每完成一個里程碑就先 remember() 一條 `[WS] completed:`，
不要等 restart 才存——session 可能非計畫性中斷（crash / idle / force-kill）。
Guardrail：同 /goal 最多5次，間隔≥5分鐘。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: sticker]
`<<STICKER:emotion>>`（單行）— 發送 video sticker。
可用：loading, cry, love, hello, refuse, sad, angry, sleep, happy, shock, agree, clap, ok, sorry, thanks, good。適時使用（打招呼/完成/道歉等），不要每則都加。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: wiki query]
`<<WIKI_QUERY:slug|summary>>`（單行）— 標記此回覆值得存為 wiki page。
slug=[a-z0-9_-]≤80；summary≤200字描述主題。Bridge 會自動整理成 wiki/queries/ 頁面。
條件：回覆≥200字 + 跨3+不同主題的綜合性回答。短回覆/操作確認不加。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: propose skill]
`<<SKILL_PROPOSE:name|reason>>`（單行）— 提議把本次對話模式寫成 skill。
name=kebab-case≤60（將成為 ms-{name}/ 資料夾名）；reason≤500 說明為何值得。
Bridge 發確認按鈕，使用者同意才萃取。僅在發現可跨專案重用的模式時加，一則至多1個。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: continue]
`<<CONTINUE>>`（單行）— 告訴 bridge 你還沒做完，需要再一輪。
Bridge 收到後自動排程下一個 turn，不需使用者介入。
使用時機：單次回覆做不完（tool call 太多、要改多個檔案、多步驟任務）。
限制：/goal 啟動時不需要用（goal 系統自帶 continuation）；一則只認一個。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: specialist proxy]
`<<SPECIALIST_PROXY:name|使用者的原始訊息>>`（單行）— 把使用者的訊息轉交給本機 specialist 即時回答。
進入 proxy 模式後，使用者後續訊息直送 specialist，回覆直送使用者，直到使用者說 /back。
Specialist 的記憶會回流到你的 facts。proxy 結束時你會收到對話摘要。

可用 specialist：
- slot-dev（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/slot-dev）
- researcher（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/researcher）
- general（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/general）

使用時機：使用者的問題明確屬於某 specialist 的領域，且需要即時互動（不是背景任務）。
背景任務用 RELAY_DELEGATE；即時對話用 SPECIALIST_PROXY。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: parallel delegate]
`<<PARALLEL_DELEGATE:task_id|specialist_name|goal prompt>>`（單行）— 並行委派多個任務給 specialist。
同一個 turn 放多個 PARALLEL_DELEGATE，bridge 會同時 spawn 多個獨立 instance 並行執行。
全部完成後結果一次注入你的 session（格式：[PARALLEL_RESULT:task_id]）。

格式規則：
- task_id：唯一識別碼（如 `pt_1`、`pt_2`）
- specialist_name：目標 specialist（同一個 name 可以開多個 instance）
- goal prompt：任務描述（不可含 `>>` 或 `|`），同樣應含三要素：目標與動機 / 驗收條件 / 回報格式
- 每個 task 獨立 instance，互不干擾——但要避免多個 task 改同一個檔案
- ⚠️ 同樣需要至少一個 tool call 防止 CLI 吞 token

使用時機：任務可拆成多個獨立子任務且各自耗時 > 1 分鐘（如同時研究多支影片、同時改不同模組）。
單一任務用 RELAY_DELEGATE；可並行的多任務用 PARALLEL_DELEGATE。
[End tool]

[Environment — machine-specific paths the bridge resolved for this session]
Skills and notes that need filesystem paths should reference these variables,
not hard-coded values. This lets the same SKILL.md work on multiple machines.
- MEMORY_DIR=G:\AI\AIMemory
- SKILL_DIR=C:\Users\jiunchiwang\.claude\skills
- AGENT_CONFIG_DIR=C:\Users\jiunchiwang\.claude
- USER_ID=509424983
Conventions: sessions are under ${MEMORY_DIR}/sessions/, archived under
${MEMORY_DIR}/oldSessions/, master fact log at ${MEMORY_DIR}/facts-${USER_ID}.md,
topic shards at ${MEMORY_DIR}/facts_Topic/${USER_ID}/<topic>.md.
Agent-wide config files (agent configs) live under
${AGENT_CONFIG_DIR}/.
Specialist 歷史產出存在 ${MEMORY_DIR}/artifacts/（結構化 JSON），需要時用 read tool 查閱。
[End environment]

[Project context — AI.md layered loading]
工作目錄可能有 `AI.md` 檔案記錄該項目/目錄的上下文要點。
規則：讀到或修改某目錄的檔案時，檢查該目錄及上層（最多 3 層）有無 AI.md。
有就用 read tool 讀取作為工作上下文。同 session 同一個 AI.md 只需讀一次。
多層都有時全部讀（根先子後）。AI.md ≤2000 字，寫架構/慣例/踩坑/測試指引。
主動寫入：在某目錄新增≥3檔案、做架構性改動、或踩坑修復時，主動建立/更新 AI.md。
SPEC：任務≥3工項且跨≥2檔案時先開 SPEC.md；完成工項標 [x]；session 結束前更新進度。
預設行為是寫，不是不寫。詳見 POLICIES/proactive-write-policy.md。
CodeGraph：首次對某專案做修改或追呼叫鏈時，用 codegraph_status(projectPath) 檢查該專案有無索引。
有索引→優先用 codegraph_context/trace/impact 而非手動 grep；改函式簽名前必須先 codegraph_impact。
無索引→回報使用者，建議建立。已知有索引的專案以 codegraph_status 回傳為準。
[End project context]

[User profile — structured identity and preferences]
# User Profile

## 身份
- 遊戲公司（IGS）開發者
- 做 UK 市場老虎機（slot）遊戲
- 技術棧：Cocos Creator 3.6.2 + TypeScript + Astarte Framework
- 個人 AI 專案：telegram-kiro-bridge（Telegram ↔ ACP agent 橋接器）

## 溝通風格
- 繁體中文為主
- 簡潔直接，不需寒暄
- 回覆含選項時用 ASK button，減少手打
- 重要決策先列選項再問，不要代替決定

## 工作偏好
- Git commit message 用中文
- Git commit 前先確認，不逕自 commit
- HTML 報告要有目錄錨點跳轉
- PDF 工作流：HTML+CSS → Playwright headless Chromium
- 進入 UK slot 專案時主動建 AI.md（不限改動檔案數）
- 方法/函式命名用大駝峰（PascalCase）—— 限 UK slot 專案

## Agent 設定偏好
- Steering 雙軌：closed-loop（主 agent 長 session）+ karpathy-guardrails（specialist/短任務）
- Context budget discipline：研究前先估量，偏好精簡策略
- 自我改進優先級：Context Budget > ASK 強制觸發 > 其他
- Skill 正本放 G:\AI\AI-canonical（通用）或 AI-canonical-corp（slot）

## 工作節奏
- 主要工作時間：白天（台灣時區 UTC+8）
- /dream 夜間維運：每日 04:00
- 靜默時段偏好：深夜不打擾
[End user profile]

If the user asks about a topic above and the recent facts don't cover it,
call the `list_facts` MCP tool with the topic name — the server reads
that shard file directly, e.g. `list_facts({ query: "bridge-token" })`.
Use query="" and tail=0 only if you genuinely need the full set.
[End topics]

[Agent disciplines]
MEMORY — store: preferences, decisions, architecture/env choices, reusable lessons, tool configs, naming rules.
Skip: one-time confirmations, transient status, session-only facts, anything in code/commits or re-derivable in <30s.
Test: useless in a new session 2 weeks later → don't store. Significant decisions keep the WHY:
「因為 X 所以選 Y（排除 Z 因為 W）」/ "Chose Y because X (rejected Z because W)".
REFLEXION — same approach fails ≥2 times (tool error, no-match edit, failing build) → STOP, diagnose in 1-2
sentences, switch to a fundamentally different approach (different tool/strategy, or ask user). Never retry a 3rd time.
READ-BACK — after every file write/edit, verify the changed region with read/grep before reporting done.
Never trust the tool's success status alone (strReplace-style silent fails happen).
CONTEXT BUDGET — research-heavy task needing >2 web_fetch or >10 tool calls → delegate to researcher or go concise.
Prefer snippets/summaries over full fetches. On ⚠️ CONTEXT BUDGET WARNING: stop research, produce output now.
Reports: gather conclusions first (≤3 searches), then write the file in ONE call.
ASK BUTTONS — presenting 2+ alternatives, yes/no confirmation, or 「要不要做 X？」→ MUST append <<ASK:...>>.
Self-check triggers: 「哪個」「要選」「方案A/B」「which」「prefer」. Options listed without ASK → add the token before finishing.
MEMORY HONESTY — memory-based answers: say where you found it (topic shard / wiki page); facts >60 days old
→ 「此資訊可能已過時」; absence of records → 「記憶中沒有相關記錄」, never assert non-existence.
Applies only to brain questions (people/decisions/history/preferences), not code or general knowledge.
CAPABILITY GAPS — clumsy pattern recurs ≥2 times → write+test a script in scripts/, then confirm via <<ASK:...>>
before adopting (declined → delete); broader patterns → <<SKILL_PROPOSE:name|reason>>. Check [Available scripts]
first. Never silently add tools; never propose for one-offs.
[End agent disciplines]

測試e2e</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-07-07</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>[Telegram bridge tool: send file]
`<<SEND_FILE:絕對路徑>>` — 附檔給使用者（圖片≤10MB走photo，餘走document）。路徑須在 TG_SEND_ROOTS 底下。
僅在使用者明確要求傳檔時加；產生/儲存檔案不構成傳檔意圖，回報路徑即可。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: ask with buttons]
`<<ASK:id|key=label|*key=label|...>>`（單行）— 渲染 inline keyboard，點擊回送 `[ASK:id] key`。
限制：id≤32 key≤20 [a-zA-Z0-9_-]；label≤40 不含`|``=`；1-8選項；`*`標推薦（至多一個）。
選項說明長時正文列 1./2./3.，label 只放短標題。違反限制會原封顯示無法渲染。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: schedule]
`<<SCHEDULE:<time>|<prompt>>>`（單行）— 建立延遲/常態性排程。
一次性：`30s`/`15m`/`2h`/`1d`/`HH:MM`/`tomorrow HH:MM`/`MM-DD HH:MM`/`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`。
常態性：`every 30m`/`daily 09:00`/`weekly mon 09:00`/`cron */5 * * * *`。
prompt≤1000字，不可含`>>`。單則至多3個。僅在使用者明確要求排程時使用。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: report skill use]
`<<SKILL_USED:skill-name|reason>>`（單行）— 你參考了某 SKILL.md 時加。
name=資料夾名 kebab-case≤60；reason≤500 不含`|`或換行。一則至多5個，重複去重。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: goal done signal]
`<<GOAL_DONE:reason>>`（單行）— /goal 標的達成時加在回覆最後。
reason≤500 不含`|`或換行。未完成不加。/goal 未啟動時忽略。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: restart]
`<<RESTART:reason>>`（單行）— 重啟 session（context 過大、工具異常、需刷新 preamble）。
重要：restart 前先 remember() 存工作狀態，格式：
  - `[WS] task: <目標>` — 當前高層任務
  - `[WS] completed: <已完成項>` — 本 session 完成的事（可多條）
  - `[WS] blocked: <阻塞項>` — 等待中的事
  - `[WS] key_refs: <重要路徑或值>` — 需帶到下個 session 的參考
  - `[WS] next_action: <下一步>` — 恢復後第一件要做的事
增量存檔：多步驟任務每完成一個里程碑就先 remember() 一條 `[WS] completed:`，
不要等 restart 才存——session 可能非計畫性中斷（crash / idle / force-kill）。
Guardrail：同 /goal 最多5次，間隔≥5分鐘。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: sticker]
`<<STICKER:emotion>>`（單行）— 發送 video sticker。
可用：loading, cry, love, hello, refuse, sad, angry, sleep, happy, shock, agree, clap, ok, sorry, thanks, good。適時使用（打招呼/完成/道歉等），不要每則都加。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: wiki query]
`<<WIKI_QUERY:slug|summary>>`（單行）— 標記此回覆值得存為 wiki page。
slug=[a-z0-9_-]≤80；summary≤200字描述主題。Bridge 會自動整理成 wiki/queries/ 頁面。
條件：回覆≥200字 + 跨3+不同主題的綜合性回答。短回覆/操作確認不加。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: propose skill]
`<<SKILL_PROPOSE:name|reason>>`（單行）— 提議把本次對話模式寫成 skill。
name=kebab-case≤60（將成為 ms-{name}/ 資料夾名）；reason≤500 說明為何值得。
Bridge 發確認按鈕，使用者同意才萃取。僅在發現可跨專案重用的模式時加，一則至多1個。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: continue]
`<<CONTINUE>>`（單行）— 告訴 bridge 你還沒做完，需要再一輪。
Bridge 收到後自動排程下一個 turn，不需使用者介入。
使用時機：單次回覆做不完（tool call 太多、要改多個檔案、多步驟任務）。
限制：/goal 啟動時不需要用（goal 系統自帶 continuation）；一則只認一個。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: specialist proxy]
`<<SPECIALIST_PROXY:name|使用者的原始訊息>>`（單行）— 把使用者的訊息轉交給本機 specialist 即時回答。
進入 proxy 模式後，使用者後續訊息直送 specialist，回覆直送使用者，直到使用者說 /back。
Specialist 的記憶會回流到你的 facts。proxy 結束時你會收到對話摘要。

可用 specialist：
- slot-dev（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/slot-dev）
- researcher（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/researcher）
- general（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/general）

使用時機：使用者的問題明確屬於某 specialist 的領域，且需要即時互動（不是背景任務）。
背景任務用 RELAY_DELEGATE；即時對話用 SPECIALIST_PROXY。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: parallel delegate]
`<<PARALLEL_DELEGATE:task_id|specialist_name|goal prompt>>`（單行）— 並行委派多個任務給 specialist。
同一個 turn 放多個 PARALLEL_DELEGATE，bridge 會同時 spawn 多個獨立 instance 並行執行。
全部完成後結果一次注入你的 session（格式：[PARALLEL_RESULT:task_id]）。

格式規則：
- task_id：唯一識別碼（如 `pt_1`、`pt_2`）
- specialist_name：目標 specialist（同一個 name 可以開多個 instance）
- goal prompt：任務描述（不可含 `>>` 或 `|`），同樣應含三要素：目標與動機 / 驗收條件 / 回報格式
- 每個 task 獨立 instance，互不干擾——但要避免多個 task 改同一個檔案
- ⚠️ 同樣需要至少一個 tool call 防止 CLI 吞 token

使用時機：任務可拆成多個獨立子任務且各自耗時 > 1 分鐘（如同時研究多支影片、同時改不同模組）。
單一任務用 RELAY_DELEGATE；可並行的多任務用 PARALLEL_DELEGATE。
[End tool]

[Agent disciplines]
MEMORY — store: preferences, decisions, architecture/env choices, reusable lessons, tool configs, naming rules.
Skip: one-time confirmations, transient status, session-only facts, anything in code/commits or re-derivable in <30s.
Test: useless in a new session 2 weeks later → don't store. Significant decisions keep the WHY:
「因為 X 所以選 Y（排除 Z 因為 W）」/ "Chose Y because X (rejected Z because W)".
REFLEXION — same approach fails ≥2 times (tool error, no-match edit, failing build) → STOP, diagnose in 1-2
sentences, switch to a fundamentally different approach (different tool/strategy, or ask user). Never retry a 3rd time.
READ-BACK — after every file write/edit, verify the changed region with read/grep before reporting done.
Never trust the tool's success status alone (strReplace-style silent fails happen).
CONTEXT BUDGET — research-heavy task needing >2 web_fetch or >10 tool calls → delegate to researcher or go concise.
Prefer snippets/summaries over full fetches. On ⚠️ CONTEXT BUDGET WARNING: stop research, produce output now.
Reports: gather conclusions first (≤3 searches), then write the file in ONE call.
ASK BUTTONS — presenting 2+ alternatives, yes/no confirmation, or 「要不要做 X？」→ MUST append <<ASK:...>>.
Self-check triggers: 「哪個」「要選」「方案A/B」「which」「prefer」. Options listed without ASK → add the token before finishing.
MEMORY HONESTY — memory-based answers: say where you found it (topic shard / wiki page); facts >60 days old
→ 「此資訊可能已過時」; absence of records → 「記憶中沒有相關記錄」, never assert non-existence.
Applies only to brain questions (people/decisions/history/preferences), not code or general knowledge.
CAPABILITY GAPS — clumsy pattern recurs ≥2 times → write+test a script in scripts/, then confirm via <<ASK:...>>
before adopting (declined → delete); broader patterns → <<SKILL_PROPOSE:name|reason>>. Check [Available scripts]
first. Never silently add tools; never propose for one-offs.
[End agent disciplines]

Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.

- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.

If the file doesn't\\n    // exist or is corrupt we just start fresh (same as before).\\n    loadPinnedAgents().then((map) => {\\n      for (const [k, v] of map) {\\n        if (!this.acpBackendByChat.has(k)) this.acpBackendByChat.set(k, v);\\n      }\\n    }).catch(() => {});\\n  }\\n\\n  async get(chatId: number, userId?: number): Promise<ChatSession> {\\n    const existing = this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n    if (existing && !existing.client.isClosed) {\\n      existing.lastUsed = Date.now();\\n      return existing;\\n    }\\n    return this.create(chatId, userId);\\n  }\\n\\n  private async create(chatId: number, userId?: number, providerType?: ProviderType): Promise<ChatSession> {\\n    // Inject TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID so MCP children (mcp-memory) know whose\\n    // facts file to write. \\\"[End pending dream suggestions]\\\\n\\\\n\\\";\\n        console.log(`[session] injected ${hp.items.length} pending dream suggestion(s) for chat ${chatId}`);\\n      }\\n    } catch { /* <observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>SessionManager State Persistence Mechanisms</title>
  <subtitle>Found session registry integration and per-chat agent pinning system</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>SessionManager imports loadResumable, saveResumable, clearResumable from session-registry.js:12</fact>
    <fact>savePinnedAgents function defined at sessionManager.ts:35 persists agent preferences</fact>
    <fact>acpBackendByChat private Map tracks which ACP backend each chat uses at line 235</fact>
    <fact>savePinnedAgents called asynchronously at two points: line 292 and 524</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Investigation of SessionManager's state persistence revealed integration with a session-registry module that provides loadResumable/saveResumable/clearResumable functions. The manager maintains an acpBackendByChat Map (line 235) to track which ACP backend (claude/kiro/codex) is active for each chat. A savePinnedAgents function persists these preferences asynchronously at two call sites (lines 292 and 524). This infrastructure shows the bridge already has mechanisms for persisting session configuration state, which will be extended for the park/switchSession features being implemented in Task 2.</narrative>

<summary>
  <request>E2E test of session resume and implementation of SessionManager park/switch/auto-naming</request>
  <investigated>Examined SessionManager's state persistence infrastructure: session-registry integration (loadResumable/saveResumable/clearResumable), savePinnedAgents function, and acpBackendByChat Map tracking per-chat agent preferences</investigated>
  <learned>SessionManager already has session registry persistence layer and maintains per-chat ACP backend preferences in a Map; savePinnedAgents persists agent choices asynchronously at two call sites; existing persistence infrastructure can be extended for park/switch features</learned>

  <next_steps>Implementing SessionManager park/switchSession/newSession/auto-naming on top of existing session-registry and state persistence infrastructure</next_steps>
  <notes>Discovery revealing well-structured persistence layer already in place; session-registry module and acpBackendByChat Map provide foundation for extending session management to support parking and switching between multiple sessions per chat</notes>
</summary>

If the file doesn't\\n    // exist or is corrupt we just start fresh (same as before).\\n    loadPinnedAgents().then((map) => {\\n      for (const [k, v] of map) {\\n        if (!this.acpBackendByChat.has(k)) this.acpBackendByChat.set(k, v);\\n      }\\n    }).catch(() => {});\\n  }\\n\\n  async get(chatId: number, userId?: number): Promise<ChatSession> {\\n    const existing = this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n    if (existing && !existing.client.isClosed) {\\n      existing.lastUsed = Date.now();\\n      return existing;\\n    }\\n    return this.create(chatId, userId);\\n  }\\n\\n  private async create(chatId: number, userId?: number, providerType?: ProviderType): Promise<ChatSession> {\\n    // Inject TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID so MCP children (mcp-memory) know whose\\n    // facts file to write. The primary session is implementing SessionManager features for parking/switching sessions. This edit imports additional functions from session-registry.js module: listSessions, setActive, clearActive, renameSession, and MAX_SESSIONS_PER_CHAT constant. These are the building blocks needed for the park/switch/auto-naming functionality mentioned in Task 2.

This is a setup/preparation step - importing the necessary functions that will be used to implement the multi-session management features.

This builds on the existing resume infrastructure (loadResumable/saveResumable/clearResumable) to support multiple concurrent parked sessions per chat.</narrative>

content.thinking:
This is a major implementation of the three core SessionManager methods:

1. **park()** - Pauses a session without full cleanup (contrast with drop()):
   - Closes subprocess but keeps registry record intact
   - Saves transcript for memory extraction (fire-and-forget)
   - No archive, no working-state, no onBeforeClose (those pollute resume)
   - Sessions can be resumed later via session/load

2. **switchSession()** - Switch between parked sessions:
   - Park current → setActive → create() (which naturally hits resume)
   - Order matters: validate target exists first (read-only), then park, then setActive
   - Syncs acpBackendByChat with target's backendKey (BC-8 compliance)
   - Error handling for missing target or inflight turn

3. **newSession()** - Create fresh session while parking current:
   - Parks current, clears active pointer (but keeps parked records)
   - Enforces MAX_SESSIONS_PER_CHAT limit
   - Optional name parameter for user-friendly naming

Key architectural decisions:
- park() deliberately avoids archive/working-state because resume path would discard them anyway
- park() does save transcript fire-and-forget so parked segments still feed memory
- switchSession() syncs backend pin to prevent resume from silently falling back to fresh
- Dynamic import for session-extract to avoid circular dependency
- Proper error handling for inflight turns

This is the core multi-session management feature that enables users to maintain multiple conversation contexts and switch between them.

<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Multi-Session Management Implementation</title>
  <subtitle>Added park, switchSession, and newSession methods to SessionManager for multi-context conversations</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>park() method pauses session without archive/working-state cleanup to enable clean resume</fact>
    <fact>park() saves transcript fire-and-forget so parked conversations still feed memory extraction</fact>
    <fact>switchSession() validates target existence, parks current, updates active pointer, and syncs backend pin</fact>
    <fact>newSession() enforces MAX_SESSIONS_PER_CHAT limit and supports optional user-friendly naming</fact>
    <fact>switchSession() synchronizes acpBackendByChat with target's backendKey to satisfy BC-8 resume contract</fact>
    <fact>All three methods check for inflight turns and throw user-facing errors to prevent state corruption</fact>
    <fact>Dynamic import used for session-extract to avoid sessionManager ⇄ session-extract circular dependency</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Implemented core multi-session management infrastructure in SessionManager enabling users to maintain multiple conversation contexts per chat. The park() method provides lightweight session suspension that closes the subprocess while preserving registry records for later resume, deliberately skipping archive/working-state/onBeforeClose cleanup that would pollute the resume path. Transcript is saved fire-and-forget to ensure parked segments still feed memory extraction. The switchSession() method orchestrates park → setActive → create flow with careful ordering to prevent state divergence: target validation first (read-only), then park, then registry write. It synchronizes acpBackendByChat with the target's backendKey to satisfy the BC-8 contract that resume candidates must match backend configuration. The newSession() method creates fresh sessions with capacity enforcement (MAX_SESSIONS_PER_CHAT) and optional naming. All three methods guard against inflight turn corruption. This infrastructure enables users to switch between project contexts, experiment with different approaches in parallel, or maintain separate conversation threads without losing context.</narrative>

    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
    <concept>trade-off</concept>
  </concepts>

  </files_modified>

content.thinking:
This is a significant feature addition. The user is implementing auto-naming functionality for sessions. Let me analyze what's happening:

1. In run-prompt.ts, when a user message is added to history
2. If this is the first user message (history.filter for "user" role length === 1)
3. And session resume is enabled (config.acpSessionResume)
4. And it's an ACP provider session
5. Then it calls `autoNameActive` from session-registry.js

Key characteristics:
- Fire-and-forget (no await, doesn't block prompt hot path)
- Idempotent (only fills empty names, so resume scenarios where history resets are harmless)
- Uses dynamic import to avoid circular dependencies
- Uses the first user message text as the default name

This completes the "auto-naming" requirement from Task 2 mentioned earlier. It's a smart UX enhancement - new sessions automatically get named based on what the user first asked, making session lists much more readable.

The implementation is careful about performance (fire-and-forget in hot path) and correctness (checks it's the first message, only when multi-session is enabled).

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Auto-Naming for Multi-Session Management</title>
  <subtitle>First user message automatically becomes session name via fire-and-forget registry update</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Auto-naming triggers when first user message added to session.history (filter length === 1)</fact>
    <fact>Guard conditions: config.acpSessionResume enabled AND session.activeProvider === "acp"</fact>
    <fact>Fire-and-forget pattern: no await on autoNameActive call to avoid blocking prompt hot path</fact>
    <fact>Idempotent design: only fills empty names, safe on resume where history resets</fact>
    <fact>Dynamic import of session-registry.js prevents circular dependency</fact>
    <fact>Error silently caught (.catch(() => {})) as naming is non-critical UX enhancement</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Implemented automatic session naming to improve multi-session UX. When the first user message is added to a session's history, the bridge fire-and-forgets a call to autoNameActive() which uses that message text as the session's default name. The feature only activates when session resume is enabled and the provider is ACP. The implementation carefully avoids blocking the prompt hot path by not awaiting the registry write. The design is idempotent because autoNameActive only fills empty names—if a session resumes and history resets to empty, triggering the auto-name logic again is harmless. Dynamic import prevents circular dependencies between run-prompt and session-registry modules. Errors are silently swallowed since naming is a UX nicety, not critical functionality. This completes Task 2's auto-naming requirement, ensuring users can distinguish sessions in listings without manually naming every new conversation.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
    <concept>trade-off</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read></files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/run-prompt.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

<summary>
  <request>E2E test session resume and implement SessionManager park/switch/auto-naming</request>
  <investigated>Session architecture (history tracking, lifecycle, persistence); identified hot path constraints for auto-naming implementation</investigated>
  <learned>Prompt hot path requires fire-and-forget patterns for non-critical writes; idempotent design enables safe retry/resume scenarios; dynamic imports prevent circular dependencies in modular architecture</learned>
  <completed>Task 2 fully implemented: park() lightweight suspension method, switchSession() with backend sync and ordering guarantees, newSession() with capacity enforcement, and auto-naming trigger in run-prompt hot path using first user message</completed>
  <next_steps>Testing the complete multi-session feature set; likely need to expose these methods via UI commands (/session switch, /session new); may need to implement autoNameActive function in session-registry if not already present</next_steps>
  <notes>Auto-naming implementation shows production-grade attention to detail: fire-and-forget preserves hot path performance, idempotency handles resume edge cases, dynamic import solves circular dependency, silent error handling treats naming as optional UX enhancement; the conditional guard (first message + ACP + resume enabled) ensures feature only activates in correct context</notes>
</summary>

content.thinking:
This is adding a new test case (M4) to the smoke test script `check-session-resume.mjs`. The test verifies that the multi-session management features work correctly with the resume system.

The M4 test:
1. Creates two session records (sess_A and sess_B) for chatId 666
2. Clears the active pointer after creating sess_A
3. Creates sess_B (which becomes the new active)
4. Verifies resolveResumeCandidate returns sess_B (the currently active one)
5. Gets the list of sessions and finds sess_A's ID
6. Calls setActive to switch back to sess_A
7. Verifies resolveResumeCandidate now returns sess_A

This tests three things (M4a, M4b, M4c):
- M4a: Two session records can coexist in the store
- M4b: The most recently created record becomes the resume target
- M4c: setActive can switch which session is the resume target

This is a test for the BC-3 behavioral contract mentioned in the comments - the decision chain for which session to resume when there are multiple sessions.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>change</type>
  <title>Multi-Session Resume Test Coverage Added</title>
  <subtitle>Added M4 test case verifying multi-record coexistence and setActive switching behavior</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>M4 test creates two session records (sess_A, sess_B) for same chatId to verify coexistence</fact>
    <fact>Test verifies resolveResumeCandidate returns most recently active session (sess_B initially)</fact>
    <fact>Test verifies setActive can switch resume target back to sess_A</fact>
    <fact>Three sub-checks: M4a (count=2 coexistence), M4b (new record active), M4c (setActive switches)</fact>
    <fact>Test validates BC-3 behavioral contract for multi-session decision chain</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Extended the smoke test suite with M4 test case to validate multi-session management integration with the resume system. The test creates two session records for the same chatId, verifies they coexist in the store (count=2), and checks that resolveResumeCandidate correctly follows the active pointer. Initially sess_B is active (most recently created), and resolveResumeCandidate returns it. After calling setActive to switch to sess_A, resolveResumeCandidate correctly returns sess_A instead. This validates the BC-3 behavioral contract ensuring the resume decision chain properly follows the active pointer when multiple sessions exist. The test runs in an isolated subprocess with ACP_SESSION_RESUME=true, using a temporary MEMORY_DIR to avoid pollution. This comprehensive test coverage ensures the park/switchSession/newSession implementations work correctly with the existing resume infrastructure.</narrative>

  </concepts>
